title: Grilled Corn and Chicken Recipes for a Quick Dinner
keyword: grilled corn and chicken recipes for dinner
meta_description: Upgrade your weeknight meals with these easy grilled corn and chicken recipes for dinner. Plus, the best tool for mess-free corn eating!
html:
There is nothing quite like the smell of a hot grill on a Tuesday evening. When you need a meal that feels like a weekend celebration but takes less than 30 minutes of active prep, combining poultry and produce on the grate is the way to go. Finding the right grilled corn and chicken recipes for dinner means balancing cook times so your chicken stays juicy while your corn gets those perfect, charred kernels.

The Secret to Synchronized Grilling

The biggest challenge with chicken and corn is the timing. A thick chicken breast takes longer than a cob of corn, but if you throw them on at the same time, you'll end up with dried-out meat or cold corn. To fix this, I recommend using a high-heat sear for the chicken first, then moving it to the indirect heat side of the grill to finish.

While the chicken is resting, that's your window for the corn. Corn cooks quickly and benefits from a direct flame. To keep things organized and safe while serving, I always reach for my Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders. These are a lifesaver because the silicone handles stay cool, and the interlocking design means I'm not hunting through a junk drawer for matching pairs before the party starts.

Honey-Lime Glazed Chicken & Charred Corn

This is my go-to recipe when I want something bright and zesty. The acidity of the lime cuts through the sweetness of the corn perfectly. For the chicken, whisk together two tablespoons of honey, the juice of one lime, a clove of minced garlic, and a pinch of salt. Marinate your breasts or thighs for at least 20 minutes.

Grill the chicken over medium-high heat until the internal temperature hits 165°F. While the meat rests, place your corn directly over the coals. Rotate every two minutes until you see those deep brown char marks. Brush the corn with melted butter and a sprinkle of chili powder for a smoky finish.

Creamy Garlic Parmesan Chicken and Corn

If you prefer something heartier, try a garlic-parmesan approach. This version works best with chicken thighs, which can handle the higher heat of the grill without drying out. Rub the chicken with olive oil, dried oregano, and plenty of cracked black pepper.

For the corn, instead of just butter, create a compound butter. Mix softened butter with grated parmesan and garlic powder. Once the corn is grilled, slather the compound butter over the cobs. Since this recipe is a bit messier due to the melted cheese and butter, using the Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders is a must. The silicone grips keep your hands clean while you navigate the cheesy goodness.

3 Tips for Better Grilled Corn and Chicken Recipes for Dinner

To elevate your grilling game, keep these practical tips in mind:

  • Dry the Meat: Pat your chicken dry with paper towels before seasoning. This ensures you get a golden-brown crust instead of steaming the meat.
  • Don't Peel Too Early: If you like a steamed effect, grill the corn in the husk for 10 minutes, then peel back the husks to char the kernels directly.
  • Rest the Protein: Always let your chicken rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring every bite is tender.

Easy Cleanup and Storage

The worst part of a quick dinner is the cleanup. When you're using a grill, the grease traps and plates can get messy. To simplify things, use a large sheet pan for your prep and a single platter for serving.

As for the tools, I love that the Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders are dishwasher safe. You can just toss them in with the rest of the cutlery. Because they lock together, they don't take up much space in the drying rack, making them an ideal addition to a crowded kitchen gadget collection.

FAQ

How do I prevent corn from sticking to the grill?

lightly brush the corn with a neutral oil or melted butter before placing it on the grates. Ensure your grill is fully preheated to medium-high heat, as food is less likely to stick to a hot surface.

Can I use these grilled corn and chicken recipes for dinner in an air fryer?

Yes! You can achieve a similar result. Air fry the chicken at 380°F until cooked through, then air fry the corn cobs for about 10-12 minutes, turning halfway through. You won't get the same smoky char, but the texture is excellent.

What are the best side dishes to pair with this meal?

Since you already have a protein and a starch, a fresh green salad or a chilled watermelon slice works best. A simple arugula salad with a lemon vinaigrette provides a peppery contrast to the sweet corn and savory chicken.
